texts: "Friars' Island is unmistakable, because it is the nearest to the ferry. Unfortunately, the origin of its name is unknown..."

elements.name: Source
texts: Curtayne, Alice, Lough Derg: St. Patrick’s Purgatory, p. 15

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourn, Ltd., London and Dublin

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "Friars' Island is unmistakable, because it is the nearest to the ferry. Unfortunately, the origin of its name is unknown. It rises higher out of the water than the others and the mound in its centre is crowned with a group of fir-trees. These mark the site of a curious improvised graveyard, where lie a number of victims of a tragic boating accident that took place in 1795."
